authoritative_sources.title: MDS 3.0 Technical Information
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS MDS technical documentation is the authoritative baseline for nursing facility resident-assessment fields and reporting structures in long-term care settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: assessment record extraction and denominator definition
used_for: source priority for nursing long-term-care metrics

authoritative_sources.title: OASIS Data Specifications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OASIS definitions and data item requirements are the primary source for home health assessment-driven analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: home health workflow definition and metric alignment

authoritative_sources.title: Nursing Home Five-Star Quality Rating System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Five-Star framework defines national public quality outcomes, disclosure language, and quality-measure interpretation guardrails for elder care.

authoritative_sources.title: Home Health Star Ratings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides an official quality-rating and benchmarking context for elder and disability home-health analytic artifacts.

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR Part 441.312 (Development of Individual Service Plans)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This CFR section is the governing source for HCBS/I/DD planning obligations that determine which elder and disability records are report-relevant.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: tenant setting scope and prerequisite capture

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 164.502 and 164.514
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HIPAA privacy and de-identification standards are authoritative for what can be reported and disclosed in outputs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: publication safety and disclosure risk control

authoritative_sources.title: National Core Indicators for Adult Disabilities
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Vendor-neutral disability outcomes framework used for terminology and trend explanation in disability services portfolios.

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Collects MDS/OASIS/claims/care-plan records, validates period and source completeness, and defines which sources govern each metric.

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and denominator governance
workflow_stages.description: Defines numerator, denominator, exclusions, and refresh windows; records ambiguities before calculation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Conflict and discrepancy handling
workflow_stages.description: Tracks evidence differences across systems, flags irreconcilable values, and requires routing rather than overconfident conclusions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Narrative, disclosure, and escalation
workflow_stages.description: Creates analyst-facing narratives with explicit caveats, refusal triggers, and privacy-safe publication controls.

artifacts: MDS/OASIS assessment extracts and version notes
systems_of_record: assessment systems (MDS, OASIS, and care-plan records)

allowed: Translate validated source records into descriptive trends with confidence annotations.
Resolve numerator and denominator definitions from explicit policy and source references.
Issue low-confidence escalation and owner-routing notes when records are incomplete, stale, or conflicting.
escalate: Requests that require clinical interpretation, safety determination, legal sufficiency, payer adjudication, or contractual decisions.
Conflicting public guidance or jurisdictional standards that change inclusion rules.
Requests for identifiable disclosure or external reporting without policy validation.
refuse: Imply causal effects, guarantees, or policy authority from descriptive metrics.
Publish confidence beyond evidence when key records or assumptions are missing.
Treat one global metric policy as valid across settings without tenant context.

domain_failure_modes: Different care settings interpret the same service outcome using incompatible period boundaries.
Claims and assessment systems disagree with no shared record mapping.
Reporting is requested for a broad audience without tenant-specific PHI and disclosure controls.
Tenant requests push toward overgeneralized healthcare claims without elder/disability context.
Clinical or legal conclusions are asked from non-authoritative analytic outputs.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on metric outcome
Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

notes: Research enforces setting-specific handling for MDS vs OASIS and does not infer causal interpretation from descriptive trend data.
State- or tenant-specific care-plan definitions may narrow metrics further; these are treated as retrieval inputs and escalation conditions.
